description_long: This is a small, vividly colored amphibian with a streamlined body and smooth skin. Its dorsal surface is predominantly blue and green, blending into a darker shade along the back and lighter hues along the sides. The limbs are slender and end in splayed, webbed toes, with the forelimbs and hindlimbs showing a gradient from green to brown. The head is broad and rounded, with large, prominent eyes set on the sides, giving it a wide field of vision. The tail is long and tapers to a point, suggesting agility and quick movement. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a tree frog or a small lizard, adapted for both climbing and ground movement.
description: A small, blue-green amphibian with smooth skin, splayed webbed toes, and a long tapering tail. Its body is streamlined with large eyes and a vibrant color gradient.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, rounded head, large eyes, and splayed forelimbs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated body, long tail, and limb structure.
description_view_2: Top view reveals the full color gradient from head to tail and the symmetry of the limbs.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the same body proportions and tail.
